sections.paragraph:
ῥυπόω (ῥύπος) 1 aor. impv. 3 sing. ῥυπωσάτω (Od. 6, 59; schol. on Apollon. Rhod. 2, 301–2a; Themist., Or. 7 p. 112, 6; Achmes 63, 8; 118, 2; Pel.-Leg. 9, 9; Pollux 4, 180; Philo, Fgm. 9 RHarris) fig. ext. of the prim. sense ‘make dirty, soil’: defile, pollute Rv 22:11 t.r. (Erasmian rdg.; s. ῥυπαίνω, ῥυπαρεύω).—DELG s.v. ῥύπος. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

sing. sing. = singular

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Themist Themist , IV a.d.—List 5

Pel.-Leg. Pel.-Leg. = Legenden der heiligen Pelagia, V? a.d.—List 5

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

ext. ext. = extension

prim. prim. = primarily

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 908
